failure_modes.text: Branching fails when teams decompose aggressively but never define how the work will be recombined into one answer.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when teams branch aggressively but never define the merge rule
failure_modes.mitigation: Define the merge protocol before branching: specify the format, criteria, and owner responsible for recombination into a single answer.

failure_modes.text: The model breaks when familiar frameworks are applied mechanically to a genuinely novel problem.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test the decomposition against a genuinely novel aspect of the problem; if the branch structure was borrowed from a past project without adaptation, challenge it.

failure_modes.text: Merge quality collapses when experts communicate in abstraction and peripheral data instead of distilling the core truth.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require each branch owner to distill their output to the core finding before merge; abstraction and peripheral detail kill integration quality.

premortem_questions.text: What is the merge rule before we create more branches?
Are these branches MECE enough that they expose the full problem without multiplying overlap?
What would show that we are forcing a familiar branch frame onto a novel problem?
premortem_questions.source_quote: apply familiar frameworks routinely and unquestioningly

heuristics.text: Decompose the problem into MECE branches before solving.
Solve each branch rigorously, but keep the future merge criterion visible while the work is still separable.
heuristics.source_quote: the Merge phase defines what emerges from that interaction

heuristics.text: Merge by explaining the answer plainly enough that the audience can follow the reasoning, not just admire the analysis.
heuristics.source_quote: write down an explanation of the concept in plain English

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on decomposition, merge discipline, and anti-brittle-framework process design.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor consulting-process sprawl because the raw source is stronger on branch discipline, merge coherence, and anti-curse-of-knowledge pressure.
open_questions: The third heuristic compresses the merge and communication sections so the intervention stays operational rather than presentation-oriented.

allies: The raw ally surface is strongest on systems-thinking, decomposition, simplification, and feynman-technique as later relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonist pressure is strongest on commitment-bias, anchoring, and curse-of-knowledge style distortion, but only clean current-vocabulary ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source has an explicit tension block with commitment-bias, which cleanly maps into the current target-id surface.
